topic: calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
method_matrix.method: The AI Scientist framework generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process for evaluation using domain-specific templates.

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
method_matrix.method: The system uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by an experiment manager agent to iteratively formulate scientific hypotheses, design and execute experiments, analyze and visualize data, and author manuscripts. It includes an enhanced AI reviewer with VLM feedback loop for iterative refinement.
method_matrix.best_result: No significant improvement in generalization performance with compositional regularization
limitations: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations.

method_matrix.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
method_matrix.method: Agent Laboratory uses a pipeline of specialized LLM agents to conduct literature review, experimentation, and report writing, operating in autonomous or co-pilot modes with human feedback integration.
limitations: The framework's quality depends on the quality of the initial research idea provided by humans
The framework may require significant computational resources
The framework's outputs might be limited by the knowledge cutoff of the LLMs used

method_matrix.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
method_matrix.method: The method involves training ML agents to predict scalar coupling constants between atom pairs using molecular structure features and additional molecular data, with evaluation based on a log-MAE metric averaged across coupling types.
method_matrix.best_result: o1-preview achieved 34.3 ± 2.4% in Low complexity category

method_matrix.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
method_matrix.method: AIBuildAI-2 initializes by collecting and organizing AI development documents into a hierarchical knowledge system, then dynamically loads relevant context for each AI task, grounds decisions in verifiable expertise, and continually evolves by distilling completed runs into structured knowledge updates.
method_matrix.best_result: No specific numeric results provided in the text

method_matrix.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
method_matrix.method: Reflexion is formalized as an iterative optimization process where agents (Actor, Evaluator, and Self-Reflection models) work together through trials until the task is completed. After each trial, the Self-Reflection model analyzes the trajectory and reward to produce a summary stored in memory, which is then used to improve future decision-making.
method_matrix.best_result: Reflexion with CoT (GT) + gpt-4 achieved 0.80 Pass@1 accuracy on HotPotQA

method_matrix.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
method_matrix.method: SELF-REFINE generates an initial output using an LLM, then iteratively refines it by having the same LLM provide feedback on its output and use this feedback to produce improved versions.
method_matrix.best_result: GPT-4 + SELF-REFINE achieves 93.1% on Math Reasoning (vs 92.9% base), with 36.2% on Sentiment Reversal (vs 3.8% base)
limitations: The paper doesn't explicitly mention limitations in the abstract

method_matrix.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
method_matrix.method: VOYAGER employs GPT-4 via blackbox queries to generate executable code for Minecraft actions, organized into a three-component system: an automatic curriculum for exploration, a skill library for storing behaviors, and an iterative prompting mechanism that refines code using environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification

method_matrix.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
method_matrix.method: The paper introduces AGENTBENCH, a benchmark consisting of 8 distinct environments that assess LLM-as-Agent's reasoning and decision-making abilities through interactive tasks.
datasets_used: Text-to-SQL datasets with queries categorized into 'Counting', 'Aggregation-MIN', 'Aggregation-MAX', 'Aggregation-AVG', 'Aggregation-SUM', 'Ranking', 'Comparison', or 'Other'
method_matrix.best_result: gpt-3.5-turbo new achieves 0.92 on UPDATE operations
limitations: The paper identifies typical reasons for failures in environments and LLMs but doesn't specify them in the abstract.

method_matrix.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
method_matrix.method: SWE-agent uses a language model agent interacting with a computer through a custom ACI that includes LM-friendly commands for navigation, file operations, and execution, with structured feedback from the environment.
datasets_used: SWE-bench test set Lite split (35 trajectories)
method_matrix.best_result: Table 7 shows % Resolved performance for task instances from different years

method_matrix.title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
method_matrix.method: RewardUQ implements four uncertainty-aware reward model architectures that predict both reward estimates and uncertainty estimates, constructing symmetric confidence bounds using these predictions.
limitations: Uncertainty-aware reward models have been adopted without thorough comparison, leaving them poorly understood
The paper focuses on evaluating uncertainty quantification methods rather than proposing new ones

method_matrix.title: Bayesian E(3)-Equivariant Interatomic Potential with Iterative Restratification of Many-body Message Passing
method_matrix.method: Implements Bayesian neural networks with E(3)-equivariant message passing, using iterative restratification and NLLJEF loss to quantify uncertainties in both energies and forces simultaneously.

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Learning for Reliable Skin Lesion Segmentation
method_matrix.method: UGDD-Net employs a dual-domain architecture with spatial and frequency branches, enhanced by three uncertainty-guided modules that actively utilize prediction uncertainty for feature fusion, graph-based refinement, and loss adaptation.
method_matrix.best_result: UGML achieved IoU of 92.61% and Dice of 96.01% on the main dataset, and IoU of 79.92% on Hard Samples

method_matrix.title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Frozen Vision-Language Models: Uncertainty Quantification with Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models
method_matrix.method: GroVE builds on Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model (GPLVM) to learn a shared low-dimensional latent space where image and text inputs are mapped to a unified representation, optimized through single-modal embedding reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives.
method_matrix.best_result: KL-Divergence achieves 0.79±0.02 for Image to Text on COCO
limitations: Existing approaches require large datasets and don't leverage pre-trained VLMs like CLIP
Deterministic embeddings from standard VLMs struggle to capture uncertainties
method_matrix.compute_required: computationally expensive compared to neural network-based methods

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-aware phase fraction prediction and active-learning-guided out-of-domain discovery of refractory multi-principal element alloys
method_matrix.method: Refractory multi-principal element alloys (RMPEAs) represent a novel class of alloys characterized by an extensive compositional design space and the potential for exceptional mechanical performance u
datasets_used: 70,000 compositions from Ti, Fe, Al, V, Ni, Nb, Zr, Mn, and Co design space
method_matrix.best_result: MDN models achieved R² values of 0.97 (FCC), 0.98 (BCC), 0.99 (Laves), 0.89 (Sigma), 0.96 (Heusler), and 0.98 (Liquid)
limitations: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations
The approach is specifically designed for phase fraction prediction in RMPEAs, which may limit generalizability to other tasks

method_matrix.title: Revisiting Unknowns: Towards Effective and Efficient Open-Set Active Learning
method_matrix.method: Trains classifier with LCE for primary head and LEDL for auxiliary head, computes purity margins using auxiliary outputs, fits GMM on logit margins to model purity regimes, and performs multi-stage selection (purity → precision → information) to select queries.

method_matrix.title: OmniDP: Beyond-FOV Large-Workspace Humanoid Manipulation with Omnidirectional 3D Perception
method_matrix.method: OmniDP processes panoramic LiDAR point clouds through a pyramid convolutional encoder enhanced with time-aware attention, producing features that condition a Diffusion Policy decoder for action generation, enabling 360° workspace awareness without base repositioning.
datasets_used: Teleoperated LiDAR point clouds + proprioceptive joint positions (29 DoFs)
method_matrix.best_result: OmniDP achieved 82/120 total success rate (16/20 Pick & Place, 12/20 Pour OV, 15/20 Pick & Place real, 12/20 Hand Over OV, 11/20 Wipe OV)
limitations: Existing approaches to expanding perception introduce mechanical complexity, calibration dependencies, and latency that hinder reliable real-time performance.

method_matrix.title: Advance warning of $\gamma$-ray blazar flares from \textit{Fermi}-LAT light curves: a strictly causal machine-learning backtest
method_matrix.method: The paper uses Bayesian Blocks to identify flare intervals, extracts 42 variability features from 365-day rolling windows, trains separate WATCH and TRIGGER models using strictly causal train-test splitting, and validates on a target source using auxiliary training sources
datasets_used: Fermi-LAT light curves for gamma-ray blazar flares
172 rolling windows (365d each, 7d shifts) from target source
method_matrix.best_result: Polynomial Logistic Regression (PLR) achieved WATCH ROC AUC = 0.891 and WATCH AP = 0.396 on held-out data
limitations: The use of strictly causal backtesting limits the amount of training data available.
The specific time windows (90 days for WATCH, 45 days for TRIGGER) might not be optimal for all types of flares.

method_matrix.title: Beyond the Loss Curve: Scaling Laws, Active Learning, and the Limits of Learning from Exact Posteriors
method_matrix.method: The authors use class-conditional normalizing flows as oracles to generate exact posterior distributions p(y|x) for realistic images, enabling decomposition of prediction error into aleatoric and epistemic components, and facilitating controlled experiments on distribution shift and active learning.
method_matrix.best_result: Epistemic error follows power-law decay (KL ∝ N^{-α}) across architectures and scales to ImageNet-1000
limitations: Uses AFHQ and ImageNet datasets rather than medical images
Computational requirements for normalizing flows may be high
Focuses on classification tasks without explicitly addressing active learning

method_matrix.title: Adaptive Outer-Loop Control of Quadrotors via Reinforcement Learning
method_matrix.method: The approach trains an optimal outer-loop policy, replaces ground-truth disturbance data with a Residual Dynamics Predictor that estimates 6D perturbations from state/action history, and uses a linear calibration bridge with online thrust correction for hardware transfer.
method_matrix.best_result: Adaptive Controller with RDP achieves RMSE of 0.024 m (0% payload)

method_matrix.title: Ask-n-Learn: Active Learning via Reliable Gradient Representations for Image Classification
method_matrix.method: Ask-n-Learn builds a calibrated classifier, estimates pseudo-labels for unlabeled data, refines low-confidence pseudo-labels via augmentation, computes gradient embeddings, and selects diverse samples using K-means++ seeding.
method_matrix.best_result: CIFAR-10: Ask-n-Learn (VWCC) achieves 70% accuracy with 10K labeled samples vs. baselines requiring nearly 20K samples
limitations: Non-calibrated uncertainties in existing active learning strategies
Insufficient trade-off between data exploration and exploitation
Presence of confirmation bias in active learning approaches

method_matrix.title: Active Output Selection Strategies for Multiple Learning Regression Models
method_matrix.method: The method uses Gaussian Processes to model multiple outputs in the same input space, with CVH selecting the output having the highest filtered cross-validation error as the leading model for query placement in each iteration.
datasets_used: Three toy examples with analytical models (sigmoid and polynomial)
method_matrix.best_result: CVH performs best in setups 1 and 2, reducing measurement points by 15-30% compared to SF

key_results.Setup 1_NRMSEval,Σ: Lowest end value with lowest standard deviation
key_results.Setup 2_NRMSEval,Σ: Best performance when nMeas ≥ 45, reduces points by 30% vs SF

limitations: Focuses on regression models, not classification models
Tested on toy examples and a benchmark dataset, not real-world calibration tasks
Doesn't address the specific challenges of medical image classification

method_matrix.title: Multi-Tier Labeling and Physics-Informed Learning for Orbital Anomaly Detection at Scale
method_matrix.method: The approach uses a three-tier labeling cascade combining physics rules (RULE), IMM-UKF, and supGP to generate labels, then trains a two-stage PIOT model on these labels using an 11-feature representation derived from TLE data.
method_matrix.best_result: PIOT-medium achieves F1=0.852 for normal class and accuracy=0.746 on test set
limitations: The physical rule set is described as 'conservative' and may miss behaviorally important anomalies
The approach relies on weak supervision sources which may have inherent biases or inaccuracies
method_matrix.compute_required: single CPU thread for IMM-UKF (seconds per satellite), GPU implied for PIOT training

method_matrix.title: Robust Conformal Outlier Detection under Contaminated Reference Data
method_matrix.method: The framework uses an outlier detection model to identify suspicious points in the contaminated reference set, then leverages a limited labeling budget to selectively annotate and remove only these points before conformal calibration.
method_matrix.best_result: Label-Trim method with Power=1.627 (±0.0501) and Type-I Error=0.009 (±0.0003) on six visual datasets at α=0.01 and r=5%
limitations: The conservativeness of conformal methods under contamination typically results in a loss of power.

method_matrix.title: Active Sequential Posterior Estimation for Sample-Efficient Simulation-Based Inference
method_matrix.method: ASNPE sequentially trains neural density estimators while selecting parameters that maximize expected divergence between posterior estimates, using an acquisition function that targets epistemic uncertainty.
datasets_used: common SBI benchmark environments (Bernoulli GLM, SLCP)
method_matrix.best_result: ASNPE outperforms other methods across most settings with better RMSNE scores using fewer simulations
limitations: Many simulation-based inference methods require large numbers of simulation samples
Methods face difficulty scaling to high-dimensional settings
Inference can be prohibitive under resource-intensive simulators

method_matrix.title: VNDUQE: Information-Theoretic Novelty Detection using Deep Variational Information Bottleneck
method_matrix.method: Trains VIB models on MNIST with held-out classes, using a Gaussian encoder with diagonal covariance and reparameterization trick, evaluating OOD detection through KL divergence and prediction entropy metrics.
method_matrix.best_result: Combined strategy achieves 95.3% average AUROC, outperforming baseline MSP by 10.3 percentage points
limitations: Maximum softmax probability (MSP) lacks theoretical grounding and suffers from miscalibration

method_matrix.title: A Machine Learning Approach Capturing Hidden Parameters in Autonomous Thin-Film Deposition
method_matrix.method: The authors developed a fully autonomous physical vapor deposition system that uses a calibration layer to measure and account for hidden parameter variations, combined with Gaussian Process Regression models and an active learning algorithm to optimize the exploration of the parameter space.
datasets_used: silver thin film deposition dataset (20,000+ data points)
baselines_compared: Control experiment without calibration layers and autonomous learning
method_matrix.best_result: Autonomous system achieved target optical properties with MAE of 0.0246 compared to 0.0618 for control experiment
limitations: Challenges in achieving fully autonomous cycles of deposition, characterization, and decision-making
Inherent sensitivity of thin film growth to hidden parameters can compromise ML model performance
method_matrix.compute_required: Not specified in the provided text

method_matrix.title: Deep-Learning Control of Lower-Limb Exoskeletons via Simplified Therapist Input
method_matrix.method: The approach uses sensor data to probabilistically infer locomotion states, allows therapists to modify these features via a user interface, and uses the adjusted locomotion features to predict desired joint posture and model stiffness in a spring-damper system based on prediction uncertainty.
datasets_used: 7 users for training, 2 healthy participants for validation
baselines_compared: transparency control (exoskeleton controlled without using self-selected features)
method_matrix.best_result: R²=0.68 between predicted and desired joint kinematics on validation set using CPM model
limitations: Extensive calibration is still required for hierarchical control approaches
User-specific parameter tuning is challenging, especially for activities like stair or ramp navigation

method_matrix.title: Gaussian Process Molecule Property Prediction with FlowMO
method_matrix.method: FlowMO builds upon GPflow and RDKit to implement Gaussian Processes for molecular property prediction using two specialized kernels: a Sub-sequence String Kernel (SSK) for SMILES string representations and a Tanimoto Kernel (TK) for fingerprint representations, enabling predictions with well-calibrated uncertainty estimates.
method_matrix.best_result: GP-SSK achieved RMSE of 0.24 on Photoswitch dataset

method_matrix.title: Murphys Laws of AI Alignment: Why the Gap Always Wins
method_matrix.method: Uses Le Cam's two-point method with transcript-level KL decomposition for lower bounds, and majority testing on flagged contexts for upper bounds, combined with tilted calculus to analyze optimization dynamics.
